Ads Campaign Report - Preliminary
Cursed Corridors: The Demon Hunts
Horror Escape Genre  |  In-Game Stats + Ads Performance  |  Data to Apr 27, 2026
Campaign 2 Apr 16-17, 2026   $20/day   Suspended by Roblox platform moderation
Campaign 3 (Active) Apr 24-30, 2026   $50/day   Objective: Maximize Plays   Daily Budget
Report Date Apr 27, 2026 (C3 still active - final report follows Apr 30)
Side Notes
  • Campaign 3 (Apr 24-30) still active - final report to follow once campaign ends
Section 1 - Executive Summary
Decision signals, key metrics, observations and actionables
Decision Signals
Ads / Marketing
MONITOR
  • Active campaign CPP at $0.0131 - within AVG range ($0.0112-$0.0229).
  • Play volume at 13,776 plays to date is sufficient to support scaling if CPP improves.
  • Play Rate at 23.9% vs 50.2% account average is the primary concern - see Play Rate note below.
  • Do not scale until Play Rate is investigated. CTR is above benchmark - creative is not the issue.
In-Game Development
NEEDS ATTENTION
  • D1 retention 0.64% vs genre 50th 7.12% - 0th percentile.
  • QPTR 0.11% vs genre 50th 1.08% - no Home Rec eligibility.
  • Playtime 5.3 min vs genre 50th 9.4 min.
  • Bug report: "pls fix the doors" - core interaction issue. Escalate immediately.
Campaign Summary
Benchmarks: CTR avg 1.88% | Play Rate avg 50.2% | CPP good ≤$0.0112 | Account avg CPP $0.0110

Note on Play Rate (23.9%)
See Ad
→
Click Ad
→
Click Play on Experience Page
→
Enters Game Successfully
  • Play Rate measures drop-off between the ad click and successfully entering a session - highlighted in the step above.
  • On genre context: Horror Escape games likely attract curiosity-driven clicks that do not always convert after the player lands on the Experience Page. A lower Play Rate than the 50.2% account average is expected for this genre.
  • No official Roblox genre-level benchmark exists for Horror Escape - 23.9% should not be treated as a red flag until comparable titles are tracked.
  • One piece of player feedback has noted the Experience Page as an area worth reviewing, though more data is needed before drawing firm conclusions.
Observations and Insights
Ads / Marketing Metrics
ObservationInsight
CTR: 2.56% - above benchmark
Benchmark - account average: 1.88%
Creatives are generating strong click intent.
  • Creative is not the issue - CTR above benchmark confirms thumbnail is working
  • Focus creative efforts on maintaining this level in the next run
Play Rate: 23.9%
Benchmark - account average: 50.2% (no genre benchmark for Horror Escape)
For every 4 clicks, approximately 1 player entered a session.
  • No genre-specific benchmark available for Horror Escape - direct comparison to account average may not be fair
  • Likely an Experience Page alignment issue
  • If Play Rate reaches account average, CPP would likely reach the GOOD threshold (≤$0.0112) without any creative change
CPP: $0.0131 - AVG range
Benchmark - good: ≤$0.0112 | AVG: $0.0112-$0.0229 | account avg: $0.0110
Play volume at 13,776 is sufficient to support scaling if efficiency improves.
  • Signal is MONITOR - volume is there, efficiency is the constraint
  • Play Rate is the single biggest lever. Improving it to account average would likely push CPP to the GOOD threshold without any budget or creative change
Word-of-mouth: New learning
Friends driving 4,789 new users with plays - 24% of total new users with plays.
  • Strong organic word-of-mouth signal from an engaged player segment
  • Improving D1 retention would compound this further
Weekend CPP: New learning
Weekend: $0.0124 | Weekday: $0.0135
-7.9% on weekends.
  • Consistent with portfolio-wide pattern (-15.5% avg)
  • Start next campaign on a Friday or Saturday
Top Creative: Creative #1 - CPP $0.011, most efficient in set
Drives 56% of total plays | CTR 2.77% (highest in set)
  • Lead with Creative #1 in the next run
  • Pause Creative #7 (CPP $0.018, lowest plays)
In-Game Metrics
ObservationInsight
D1 Retention: 0.64% - 0th percentile
Benchmark - genre 50th: 7.12% | genre 90th: 11.86%
Players are not returning the next day.
  • Most critical product signal
  • Scaling spend without fixing this means paying for players who don't come back
QPTR: 0.11% - 9th percentile
Benchmark - genre 50th: 1.08% | genre 90th: 2.34%
  • No Home Recommendation eligibility
  • Organic discovery will remain minimal until QPTR improves
Avg Playtime: 5.3 min
Benchmark - genre 50th: 9.4 min | genre 90th: 23.2 min
  • Players are leaving before meaningful depth
  • Is the core loop engaging enough within the first 5 minutes?
Bug Report: "pls fix the doors"
1 downvote - Apr 26, 2026
  • Flag to dev team for initial investigation - based on 1 review, verify before treating as confirmed
  • If confirmed: broken core interaction in a horror escape game that directly impacts D1 retention and playtime
Actionables
Owner Priority Action
Ads / Publishing MONITOR
Budget
  • Do not scale budget at this stage
  • Priority 1 constraint: Developers to investigate in-game metrics - D1 retention at 0.64% and QPTR at 0.11% need to improve before additional spend will compound effectively
  • Priority 2 constraint: Publishing to investigate Play Rate drop-off
HIGH
Experience Page
  • Review Experience Page assets - description, screenshots, and video should match the ad creative atmosphere
NORMAL
Next Campaign
  • Continue Creative #1 as lead
  • Pause Creative #7 (CPP $0.018) in the next run
  • Start next campaign on a Wednesday - the platform typically takes 1-2 days to exit its learning phase, allowing full delivery by Friday into the weekend (weekend CPP -7.9% vs weekday)
Development HIGH
Bug Fix
  • Fix the doors bug - broken core interaction in a horror escape game
  • Directly impacts D1 retention and playtime
  • Note: Based on 1 downvote - dev team to investigate and verify this first before prioritising a fix
HIGH
First-Session Depth
  • Investigate first-session drop-off
  • Avg playtime at 5.3 min vs genre 50th 9.4 min
  • What is causing players to exit before meaningful depth?
HIGH
D1 Retention
  • D1 retention at 0.64% vs genre 50th 7.12% - 0th percentile
  • Most critical product metric
  • Scaling spend without improving this means paying for players who don't come back
Final report to follow once campaign ends Apr 30.
End of Section 1
